title: Final 2 episodes
wikitext:
[]: Final 2 episodes             Quote Post by amandaknox » Sat Sep 20, 2014 8:20 am I think most of us would have come up with our own conclusions, the impression I got was that the world had ended and it was the Armageddon judgement day, but it was focused on Shinji and Misato. Took me a while to come to that conclusion as at first I thought it was Shinji's mind going mental. Anyone else have a hypothesis on this?

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Sat Sep 20, 2014 8:34 am A hypothesis of what? I think you're asking "if you didn't know it was third impact in the final two episodes, what would/did you think was going on?"? But you could be asking for a hypothesis on why you thought it was his "mind going mental". I think the second is a more interesting thing to ask of others, but I doubt it's the case. I'll wait and see.

[]:             Quote Post by amandaknox » Sat Sep 20, 2014 8:40 am I'm referring to the flashbacks, questions and the analysing of their lives. Whether it be another take on life flashing before their eyes, a happening in the afterlife and an angel or another being getting into their minds.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Sat Sep 20, 2014 8:52 am Given your wording, I'm going to assume you're not aware it's the human instrumentality project , brought about by third impact. Basically, in the final two episodes, humanity has been forced into a new state of being whereby everyone is now a single entity with shared consciousness, and what we see is the character's reaction to this experience; how they see themselves and others, and how others see them. It's not about what we think is going on here - there actually is an explanation. If you haven't seen End of Evangelion yet, I suggest you do; it makes it all much more comprehendible.

[]: Re: Final 2 episodes             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Sep 20, 2014 10:38 am amandaknox wrote: Anyone else have a hypothesis on this?

[]:             Quote Post by amandaknox » Sat Sep 20, 2014 10:43 am It confused it all the more for me when somebody told me that the last 2 episodes were thrown together once the budget ran low and the movie was meant to replace them. From the world ending in the movie I assumed that the series skipped it happening (based on what I was told) and went straight to judgement day. To be totally honest with you I'm having a little trouble deciphering a lot of it, I still don't fully understand who Rei is. When I first watched this at the age of 15 I thought it turned out she was a man-made reincarnation of Yui, but now that I've watched it again (12 years later) I'm starting to wonder if she's meant to be God or something

[]:             Quote Post by Jäeger » Sun Sep 21, 2014 7:30 am amandaknox wrote: It confused it all the more for me when somebody told me that the last 2 episodes were thrown together once the budget ran low and the movie was meant to replace them. From the world ending in the movie I assumed that the series skipped it happening (based on what I was told) and went straight to judgement day. To be totally honest with you I'm having a little trouble deciphering a lot of it, I still don't fully understand who Rei is. When I first watched this at the age of 15 I thought it turned out she was a man-made reincarnation of Yui, but now that I've watched it again (12 years later) I'm starting to wonder if she's meant to be God or something

[]:             Quote Post by amandaknox » Sun Sep 21, 2014 7:49 am Was Yui always god or was Rei made from elements of the two?

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Sun Sep 21, 2014 9:44 am amandaknox wrote: Was Yui always god or was Rei made from elements of the two?

[]: Re: Final 2 episodes             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Sep 21, 2014 12:15 pm NemZ wrote: Do a search for "Concurrency" if you want to know more, but I'll warn you it's quite a mess.

[]: Re: Final 2 episodes             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 21, 2014 1:41 pm amandaknox wrote: Anyone else have a hypothesis on this?

[]:             Quote Post by Jäeger » Thu Sep 25, 2014 2:47 am Sorrow wrote: Rei's "godhood" doesn't come from Yui; she has the soul of the second Angel, Lilith. It's easiest to just consider Rei as Lilith turned human. Rei's body is a clone of Yui, not Rei herself. Though, given the number of Reis there are and all of the little legs sprouting from where Lilith's used to be, I think many assume the clones to have grown from Lilith. So it's hard to tell how much of Yui's DNA is used in the creation of Rei - certainly enough for her to resemble her a great deal; though any other DNA she may possess would certainly have come from Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by amandaknox » Thu Sep 25, 2014 2:53 am In EoE she appeared before everyone as they died, does this make her the reeper too? And Kaworu was an angel, but also said he was a representation of love, wonder if every angel represented something, in that case would Rei be the angel of death?

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Thu Sep 25, 2014 7:42 am amandaknox wrote: And Kaworu was an angel, but also said he was a representation of love, wonder if every angel represented something, in that case would Rei be the angel of death?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Sep 25, 2014 7:54 am Sorrow wrote: given the number of Reis there are and all of the little legs sprouting from where Lilith's used to be, I think many assume the clones to have grown from Lilith. So it's hard to tell how much of Yui's DNA is used in the creation of Rei - certainly enough for her to resemble her a great deal; though any other DNA she may possess would certainly have come from Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Thu Sep 25, 2014 11:20 am NemZ wrote: Not necessarily. Its possible they just seeded Lilith's innert biomass with salvaged genetic material from Yui and it spontantiously began to replicate repurposing the existing tissue, like a cancer. Rei doesn't necessarilly have to have any of Lilith's DNA at all... her weirdness could be just due to being made of angel stuff rather than normal flesh.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Sep 25, 2014 1:10 pm amandaknox wrote: In EoE she appeared before everyone as they died, does this make her the reaper too?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Sep 25, 2014 4:12 pm NemZ wrote: Not necessarily. Its possible they just seeded Lilith's innert biomass with salvaged genetic material from Yui and it spontantiously began to replicate repurposing the existing tissue, like a cancer. Rei doesn't necessarilly have to have any of Lilith's DNA at all... her weirdness could be just due to being made of angel stuff rather than normal flesh.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Sep 25, 2014 5:52 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: I never considered it since I never believed there to be signs pointing to it.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Sep 25, 2014 6:14 pm True. I considered it with the Eva growth in mind, but still came away not liking the theory.
summary:
[]: Final 2 episodes             Quote Post by amandaknox » Sat Sep 20, 2014 8:20 am I think most of us would have come up with our own conclusions, the impression I got was that the world had ended and it was the Armageddon judgement day, but it was focused on Shinji and Misato. Took me a while to come to that conclusion as at first I thought it was Shinji's mind going mental. Anyone else have a hypothesis on this?
